hindiTranslation: उसके बाद पृथानन्दन अर्जुनने उन दोनों ही सेनाओंमें स्थित पिताओंको, पितामहोंको, आचार्योंको, मामाओंको, भाइयोंको, पुत्रोंको, पौत्रोंको तथा मित्रोंको, ससुरोंको और सुहृदोंको भी देखा ॥
englishTranslation: Then Arjuna, son of Pritha, saw fathers, grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, sons, grandsons, friends, fathers-in-law, and well-wishers standing there.
speaker: Sanjaya
simpleMeaning: Arjuna looks across the battlefield and recognizes his own relatives and teachers among the enemy ranks.
simpleInsight: The enemy is no longer abstract. The sight of familiar faces turns battle into grief.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Krishna has placed Arjuna between the armies. As Arjuna looks, he sees fathers, grandfathers, teachers, cousins, sons, and friends on both sides. The war becomes personal in a single glance.
modernRelevance: You open a group chat before a painful family decision and see every name tied to memory. The choice is no longer theoretical; it cuts through your own relationships.
translationLiteral: तत्र (there) / अपश्यत् (saw) / स्थितान् (standing) / पार्थः (Arjuna, son of Pritha) / पितृ़न् (fathers) / अथ (also) / पितामहान् (grandfathers) / आचार्यान् (teachers) / मातुलान् (maternal uncles) / भ्रातृ़न् (brothers) / पुत्रान् (sons) / पौत्रान् (grandsons) / सखीन् (friends) / तथा (and so on)
